priorityScale.P0: Serious: plausible production compromise, public mutation surface, or integrity break; resolve before beta/production unless explicitly accepted.
priorityScale.P1: Concerning: material exposure or abuse path; reduce before beta when feasible and block production unless acknowledged.
priorityScale.P2: Risky: hardening, minimization, or defense-in-depth gap; track with a concrete owner and release decision.
priorityScale.P3: Warning: hygiene or monitoring issue; keep visible so future code review does not reintroduce it.

productionGatePolicy.summary: Production publish is blocked when a tracked security issue is open and not explicitly acknowledged, or when a tracked issue is marked resolved but is still observed in the production artifact.
productionGatePolicy.betaBehavior: Beta publish shows tracked issues as reminders only.

issues.title: Production public artifact exposes reference audio assets
issues.summary: Local beta and production artifacts no longer expose public hrefs to private reference-audio clips or bundle private clip bytes. Hosted public Pages cleanup on 2026-06-10 removed stale root, beta, and dev reference-audio files; known historical clip URLs now return 404 instead of audio bytes.
evidence: Live production page referenced assets/reference-audio/*.m4a.
All 24 referenced public reference-audio paths checked during the passive review returned HTTP 200.
GitHub Pages responses included access-control-allow-origin: *.
Current local beta and production security reviews report SEC-001 not observed after public-lane runtime, application-guide, project-guide, and source-preview stripping.
After public Pages commit 6f50151, known historical root, /beta, and /dev reference-audio clip URLs return HTTP 404 text/html instead of audio/mp4.
proposedPlan: Keep local/dev reference-audio provisioning through ignored local assets or private artifact bootstrap only.
Keep public-lane runtime and generated docs stripped of private reference-audio hrefs.
Keep artifact-boundary checks active so production fails if dist/production references or contains private reference-audio paths.
Keep the publish-lane prune guard active for root, /beta, and /dev assets/reference-audio directories.
issues.nextAction: Keep the publish-lane prune guard active and recheck known historical clip URLs after future hosted lane publishes.
issues.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifacts contain no reference-audio clip bytes and no public href/text path that invites fetching private reference clips; dev/local review keeps private provisioning explicit and ignored.

issues.title: Production bundle exposes harness and debug mutation APIs
issues.summary: Local beta and production artifacts no longer expose browser harness globals, force-write harness symbols, or debug crash globals. Dev still carries the harness for local automation.
evidence: Public bundle includes window.__galagaHarness__ and aliases it to window.__platinumHarness__.
Harness methods include stage start, controlled clock advancement, score awarding, forced game over, remote score test setup, local replay seeding, and debug state reads.
Production bundle also contained force-write harness flag names.
Current local beta and production security reviews report SEC-002 not observed after public-lane harness export stripping.
proposedPlan: Keep browser harness exports only in dev/test lanes.
Strip public-lane harness export blocks and debug crash globals during production build and beta promotion.
Keep production artifact checks for window.__galagaHarness__, window.__platinumHarness__, and force-write harness flag names.
Keep remote write force flags hard-disabled in production even if a public user defines similarly named globals.
issues.nextAction: Keep the public-lane scan active and run a dev harness smoke check after any future harness/build-lane change.
issues.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifact scans no longer find harness globals or force-write symbols, while local/dev harness checks still pass.

issues.title: Leaderboard write integrity depends on client-trusted score submission
issues.summary: Public beta and production artifacts no longer ship the client-side score insert path. Remote score writes are disabled in public lanes pending server-side validation or a documented RLS policy audit; dev/local harness score-write testing remains available.
evidence: Public bundle includes SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY.
Public bundle inserts into the scores table from client-side score/game-over state.
Read-only passive checks confirmed scores and profiles are publicly selectable with the anon key.
No service-role key was observed in the public bundle.
Current local beta and production security reviews report SEC-003 not observed after public-lane score-submit stripping.
proposedPlan: Keep public beta/production score writes disabled until score acceptance is server-validated.
Audit Supabase RLS for scores and profiles before re-enabling public writes.
Require authenticated users to write only their own user_id and prevent client-controlled verified fields.
Move score acceptance to a server-side RPC or edge function that validates a signed run summary or replay evidence where practical.
issues.nextAction: Before restoring online score writes in beta/production, capture the Supabase RLS/policy state and implement a server-validated score acceptance path.
issues.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifacts do not ship direct client score inserts, or a release artifact records server-side/RLS validation that prevents client-controlled trusted fields.

issues.title: Hosted production lacks explicit browser security headers
issues.summary: Local beta and production HTML artifacts now include a generated meta Content-Security-Policy and strict-origin-when-cross-origin referrer policy. GitHub Pages HTTP-header hardening remains a future hosting improvement.
evidence: Passive header check on the live production URL showed GitHub Pages defaults and no obvious app-owned hardening headers.
The app is a large inline JS bundle and uses many innerHTML rendering paths, so lack of CSP increases blast radius if any injection bug appears.
Current local beta and production security reviews report SEC-004 not observed after generated meta CSP/referrer policy insertion.
proposedPlan: Keep the generated meta CSP and referrer policy on beta/production HTML artifacts.
Track the residual GitHub Pages limitation that frame-ancestors, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, and Permissions-Policy need real host headers.
When feasible, front the site with a host that can set app-owned security headers.
Reduce unsafe-inline CSP allowances as build packaging moves away from inline bundle/style delivery.
issues.nextAction: After the next hosted beta/dev publish, verify the meta CSP is present in live HTML and capture the remaining host-header limitations.
issues.doneDefinition: The active host emits documented hardening headers or the app ships the strictest feasible meta policy with explicit residual GitHub Pages limitations.

issues.title: Production build metadata exposes test pilot emails and internal release context
issues.summary: Local beta and production artifacts no longer expose non-production test pilot emails or user ids in build-info, release-notes, or inline build metadata. Dev/local review keeps the test-pilot configuration.
evidence: build-info.json includes platform.auth.nonProductionTestPilotEmails.
The production bundle includes the same configured test pilot emails.
build-info.json also exposes commit, branch, promoted-from state, build timing, and release-review context.
Current local beta and production security reviews report SEC-005 not observed after public-safe release-manifest projection and beta JSON sanitization.
proposedPlan: Keep non-production test pilot emails out of beta/production build-info, release-notes, and inline build metadata.
Keep dev/local test-pilot configuration available through the canonical release manifest.
Keep metadata minimization checks in the production publish preflight.
Review remaining commit/branch/build timing metadata separately if release authority wants stricter public minimization.
issues.nextAction: Keep the public-lane email scan active and verify hosted beta/production after the next publish.
issues.doneDefinition: Beta and production build-info, release-notes, and inline build metadata contain no non-production test pilot emails/user ids, while dev/local auth review still has the needed test-pilot configuration.

issues.title: Production bundle mixes dev, beta, and production account/test logic
issues.summary: Local beta and production artifacts now strip public harness/debug exports, strip client score writes, clear test-pilot constants, and hard-disable the account-review lane with NON_PRODUCTION_LANE=false.
evidence: Production bundle includes TEST_ACCOUNT_EMAILS, NON_PRODUCTION_LANE, testAccountEnabled, and force-write harness flag names.
Auth and score behavior branches by release lane inside the public runtime instead of being stripped by build lane.
Current local beta and production security reviews report SEC-006 not observed after public-lane hard-disable and stripping.
proposedPlan: Keep lane-specific dev/test controls stripped or hard-disabled in beta/production at build/promotion time.
Keep production account and leaderboard logic minimal and explicit.
Keep the production bundle scan for active dev/test-only symbols.
Document any intentionally retained diagnostics as read-only support surfaces.
issues.nextAction: Keep the hard-disable scan active and add a focused public-lane account smoke before re-enabling any beta test-pilot behavior.
issues.doneDefinition: Production bundle scans no longer find dev/test-only lane symbols unless they are documented as inert public support text; dev/beta behavior remains covered by harnesses.
